presentation.chiefComplaint: Tired, nauseated, and breathing a bit fast

history.hpi: A young adult with a day of fatigue, nausea, and a couple of episodes of vomiting that he blames on a stomach bug. On questioning he has been extremely thirsty, urinating frequently, and his breathing feels deep and rapid. A friend thought his breath smelled fruity. He recently ran out of his insulin.
medications: Insulin (ran out a few days ago)
history.socialHistory: College student; no alcohol or drugs today.
redFlags: Diabetic ketoacidosis: polyuria, polydipsia, vomiting with insulin omission
Kussmaul respirations — deep rapid breathing is respiratory compensation, not anxiety
Needs immediate glucose, ketones, and IV fluids

redFlagConcepts.flag: Diabetic ketoacidosis: polyuria, polydipsia, vomiting with insulin omission
Kussmaul respirations — deep rapid breathing is respiratory compensation, not anxiety
Needs immediate glucose, ketones, and IV fluids

expert.esiRationale: The trap: 'tired and nauseated, probably a stomach bug' with a near-normal blood pressure and SpO2 invites ESI 3. But a type-1 diabetic who ran out of insulin, with polyuria, polydipsia, fruity breath, a markedly elevated glucose, and a danger-zone respiratory rate (28 = Kussmaul respiration) is in diabetic ketoacidosis — high-risk and time-critical for fluids and an insulin infusion. ESI 2. The tachypnea is the key objective danger-zone vital a good triage will measure and recognize.

provenance.sourceRef: ED Triage Trainer hand-authored calibration-trap case
